message:
refactor(vendored): unify mux I/O through runtime_compat::io, expand async contract coverage

mux_client + mux_pool: Replace the two duplicated, cfg-gated
unix_stream_read implementations (one for asupersync-runtime, one for
tokio) with a single function that delegates to the runtime_compat::io
abstraction. This removes direct asupersync::io::ReadBuf usage and
AsyncReadExt fallback, centralizing the read path.

mux_pool: Extract write_response_pdu helper that uses Pdu::encode_async
instead of manually encoding to a Vec and writing — reducing boilerplate
in three mock server loops and avoiding an unnecessary intermediate
allocation.

vendored_async_contracts: Add compatibility_mapped_contract_ids() and
compatibility_unmapped_verifiable_contract_ids() audit functions that
compute contract coverage across the compatibility matrix. Expand
standard_compatibility_mappings to map additional contracts (ABC-OWN-002,
ABC-ERR-001, ABC-CAN-002, ABC-TL-002) so every verifiable contract is
now accounted for.

Tests: Add V25b verification test confirming the compatibility matrix
covers all verifiable contract IDs. Add proptest and integration tests
ensuring no verifiable contract goes unmapped and all expected categories
are represented. Format tokio runtime builder calls consistently.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
